channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [DUCON] Ducon Board Approves Share Capital Increase
item.description:
Ducon Infratechnologies Ltd's board has approved an increase in authorized share capital and the conversion of unsecured loans from Promoter and Managing Director, Mr. Arun Govil, into equity shares, subject to shareholder approval. An Extraordinary General Meeting is scheduled for May 20, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for these resolutions. In FY25, Ducon reported consolidated Total Income of ₹451 Cr, EBITDA of ₹31 Cr, and Net Profit of ₹14 Cr.

  • Ducon Board approves increase in authorized share capital, pending shareholder approval.
  • Promoter loan conversion into equity approved, reinforcing balance sheet.
  • Extraordinary General Meeting scheduled for May 20, 2026, for shareholder approvals.
  • FY25 consolidated Total Income reported at ₹451 Cr.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ducon Infratechnologies Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [AVANTEL] Avantel Appoints Grandhy & Co. as Statutory Auditors, New Directors
item.description:
Avantel Ltd's Board of Directors, at a meeting on April 26, 2026, approved several key changes. The company appointed M/s. Grandhy & Co., Chartered Accountants, as its Statutory Auditors for a five-year term, subject to shareholder approval. Additionally, M/s. MPR & Associates were appointed as Cost Auditor and M/s. Ramesh & Co. as Internal Auditor for the financial year ending March 31, 2027. The Board also appointed Dr. Tamilmani Kandasamy and Mr. Lakshminarasimha Acharyulu Muktevi as Additional Non-Executive Independent Directors for a five-year period. Furthermore, the company announced the reconstitution of its Audit, Nomination & Remuneration, Stakeholders Relationship, and Corporate Social Responsibility Committees.

  • Avantel appointed Grandhy & Co. as Statutory Auditors for five years.
  • Two independent directors, Dr. Tamilmani Kandasamy and Mr. Lakshminarasimha Acharyulu Muktevi, were appointed.
  • Cost and Internal Auditors were also appointed for FY27.
  • Four key Board Committees, including the Audit Committee, were reconstituted.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: AVANTEL LTD. | Category: Auditor

item.title: [AVANTEL] Avantel Appoints Additional Directors & Statutory Auditors
item.description:
Avantel Ltd's board, in its meeting on April 26, 2026, approved the appointment of Dr. Tamilmani Kandasamy and Mr. Lakshminarasimha Acharyulu Muktevi as Additional Directors (Non-Executive - Independent) for a 5-year term. The board also approved the reconstitution of various committees and the appointment of M/s. Grandhy & Co. as Statutory Auditors for five years, subject to member approval. Additionally, M/s. MPR & Associates were appointed as Cost Auditor and M/s. Ramesh & Co. as Internal Auditor for the financial year ending March 31, 2027.

  • Dr. Tamilmani Kandasamy appointed as Additional Director (Independent) for 5 years.
  • Mr. Lakshminarasimha Acharyulu Muktevi appointed as Additional Director (Independent) for 5 years.
  • M/s. Grandhy & Co. appointed as Statutory Auditors for 5 years, subject to AGM approval.
  • M/s. MPR & Associates appointed as Cost Auditor for FY ending March 31, 2027.
  • M/s. Ramesh & Co. appointed as Internal Auditor for FY ending March 31, 2027.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: AVANTEL LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [AVANTEL] Avantel Approves FY26 Results, Recommends ₹0.20 Dividend
item.description:
Avantel Ltd's board approved the audited financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. They recommended a final dividend of ₹0.20 per equity share (10%) for FY26, subject to shareholder approval. The board also approved the appointment of Dr. K. Tamilmani and Mr. Lakshminarasimha Acharyulu Muktevi as Additional Directors and reconstituted various committees. Furthermore, they approved increasing borrowing limits to ₹350 crore and appointed M/s. Grandhy & Co. as Statutory Auditors, M/s. MPR & Associates as Cost Auditor and M/s. Ramesh & Co. as Internal Auditor.

  • Audited FY26 financial results (standalone and consolidated) approved.
  • Final dividend of ₹0.20 per share (10%) recommended for FY26.
  • Dr. K. Tamilmani and Mr. Lakshminarasimha Acharyulu appointed as Additional Directors.
  • Borrowing limits increased from ₹200 crore to ₹350 crore, subject to shareholder approval.
  • M/s. Grandhy & Co. appointed as Statutory Auditors for five years, subject to member approval.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: AVANTEL LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [ACMESOLAR] ACME Solar Board Meeting on May 7, 2026 to Approve Results
item.description:
ACME Solar Holdings Ltd has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on May 7, 2026, to consider and approve the audited financial results (Standalone and Consolidated) for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The trading window for dealing in the company's securities has been closed from April 1, 2026, and will remain closed until 48 hours after the declaration of the audited financial results.

  • ACME Solar board meeting set for May 7, 2026, to approve audited financial results.
  • Meeting will cover standalone and consolidated results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026.
  • Trading window closed from April 1, 2026, until 48 hours post results declaration.
  • The company's insider trading code governs the trading window closure.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ACME Solar Holdings Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [AVANTEL] Avantel Ltd Announces FY26 Audited Results; Dividend of ₹0.20
item.description:
Avantel Ltd's Board of Directors approved the audited financial results for FY 2025-26, along with a final dividend of ₹0.20 per equity share (10%) subject to shareholder approval. The board also approved the appointment of Dr. K. Tamilmani and Mr. Lakshminarasimha Acharyulu Muktevi as Additional Directors. Furthermore, the board approved enhancement of borrowing limits from ₹200 crore to ₹350 crore and recommended the appointment of M/s. Grandhy & Co. as Statutory Auditors. The 36th AGM will be held on June 24, 2026, via video conferencing.

  • Avantel's board approved audited financial results for FY 2025-26.
  • A final dividend of ₹0.20 per share (10%) was recommended, pending shareholder approval.
  • Dr. K. Tamilmani and Mr. Lakshminarasimha Acharyulu Muktevi appointed as Additional Directors.
  • Borrowing limits increased from ₹200 crore to ₹350 crore, subject to shareholder approval.
  • M/s. Grandhy & Co. recommended as Statutory Auditors for five years.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: AVANTEL LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [BUTTERFLY] Butterfly Appliances: Consumer Commission Order - Product Defect
item.description:
Butterfly Gandhimathi Appliances Ltd received an order from the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission, Jeyepore, Odisha, regarding a defect alleged in an electric chimney. The company is directed to refund ₹22,000 with 6% interest from the purchase date (09.09.2023), pay ₹15,000 as compensation, and ₹5,000 for litigation costs. The company states that this order will not have a material impact on its financial or operational activities.

  • Consumer Commission orders refund of ₹22,000 plus interest for defective electric chimney.
  • Butterfly Appliances to pay ₹15,000 as compensation to the consumer.
  • Litigation costs of ₹5,000 also to be borne by the company.
  • The company claims no material impact on financials or operations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Butterfly Gandhimathi Appliances Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [CDG] CDG Petchem EGM Approves Name Change
item.description:
CDG Petchem Ltd's Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) held on April 24, 2026, approved the change of the company's name and consequent alterations to its Memorandum and Articles of Association. The remote e-voting results showed strong support for the resolution, with 3,160,195 votes in favor and only 1 vote against. Akash & Co., Company Secretaries, served as the scrutinizer for the meeting.

  • CDG Petchem EGM held on April 24, 2026, approved the change of company name.
  • Resolution passed with 3,160,195 votes in favor and 1 vote against.
  • Promoter group e-voting showed 1,911,052 votes in favor, representing 28.06% of outstanding shares.
  • Public non-institutions e-voting showed 1,249,143 votes in favor and 1 vote against.
  • Akash & Co., Company Secretaries, was the scrutinizer for the EGM.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: CDG Petchem Ltd | Category: AGM/EGM

item.title: [ARMAN] Arman Holdings: Special Window for Physical Share Dematerialisation
item.description:
Arman Holdings Ltd has announced a special window from February 5, 2026, to February 4, 2027, for physical shareholders to transfer and dematerialize their shares. This initiative, pursuant to SEBI Circular No. HO/38/13/11(2)2026-MIRSD-POD/ 1/3750/2026, is available to shareholders whose transfer requests lodged before April 1, 2019, were rejected due to deficiencies. Shareholders are encouraged to submit required documents to Adroit Corporate Services Private Limited. Shares re-lodged for transfer will be issued in demat form only. The circular is available on the company's website.

  • Arman Holdings opens special window for physical share dematerialisation.
  • Window open from February 5, 2026, to February 4, 2027.
  • Applies to requests before April 1, 2019, rejected due to deficiencies.
  • Shares will be issued in demat form upon re-lodgement.
  • Adroit Corporate Services is the Registrar and Transfer Agent.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Arman Holdings Ltd | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [JKCEMENT] JK Cement Declared Preferred Bidder for Limestone Block
item.description:
JK Cement Ltd has been declared the 'Preferred Bidder' for the Mining lease of Maliyakheri Limestone Block-I (spreads over an area of 6.90 hectares) in Chittorgarh, Rajasthan, following an e-auction conducted by the Government of Rajasthan. The company received the information on April 25, 2026, at around 3 PM (IST).

  • JK Cement wins bid for Maliyakheri Limestone Block-I.
  • The block spans 6.90 hectares in Chittorgarh, Rajasthan.
  • The e-auction was conducted by the Government of Rajasthan.
  • Information received by the company on April 25, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: J.K.CEMENT LTD. | Category: Business Update
